note: CURATED SYNTHETIC task set for the AIN-459 / Tap-3 integration cycle. These prompts are author-written, public-safe, and DO NOT derive from any real customer traffic — customer prompt text is NOT persisted in the schema, so a real-traffic freeze (loader.freeze_from_rows) has no data source yet. Numbers produced from this set are a PIPELINE proof only — NOT a measured real-traffic result. Any snapshot built from this set is tagged state="illustrative" and must NEVER be published to /proof or read as measured. Stage-2 statistical run still requires >=200 real tasks/type (config.MIN_TASKS_PER_TYPE); this 20/type set will log an 'undersized' warning, which is expected for an integration cycle.

tasks.prompt: Write a Python function that merges two sorted lists into one sorted list without using sorted().
Given this snippet, find and fix the off-by-one error in the for-loop that iterates over indices.
Refactor this deeply nested if/else chain into an early-return style for readability.
Implement a debounce decorator in JavaScript that delays a function call by N milliseconds.
Write a SQL query that returns the top 5 customers by total order value in the last 30 days.
Add input validation to this REST handler so it rejects requests with a missing or non-integer 'id'.
Convert this synchronous file-reading function to an async version using asyncio.
Write a unit test for a function that returns the nth Fibonacci number, covering edge cases 0 and 1.
Explain why this recursive function causes a stack overflow on large inputs and rewrite it iteratively.
Implement a least-recently-used (LRU) cache with a fixed capacity in Python using OrderedDict.
Given this Dockerfile, reduce the final image size by switching to a multi-stage build.
Write a regular expression that matches valid IPv4 addresses and reject 256+ octets.
Parse this CSV string into a list of dictionaries keyed by the header row.
Add structured logging to this function so each branch logs at the appropriate level.
Write a function that flattens an arbitrarily nested list of integers into a single flat list.
Fix the race condition in this counter that is incremented from multiple threads.
Implement pagination for this database query using keyset (cursor) pagination instead of OFFSET.
Write a bash script that finds and deletes files older than 7 days in a given directory.
Convert this callback-based API client into one that returns Promises.
Memoize this expensive pure function and explain when the cache should be invalidated.
Summarize this customer support thread into three bullet points capturing the issue, cause, and resolution.
Condense this 1,200-word changelog into a two-sentence release note for end users.
Summarize the key risks identified in this quarterly incident report for an executive audience.
Reduce this meeting transcript to a list of decisions made and owners assigned.
Write a one-paragraph abstract for this technical design document.
Summarize the main argument of this opinion article in neutral, non-editorial language.
Condense these five product reviews into a single balanced summary of pros and cons.
Summarize this legal contract clause in plain language a non-lawyer can understand.
Provide a TL;DR of this long email chain about the deployment delay.
Summarize the findings of this user-research study into three actionable insights.
Compress this onboarding guide into a quick-start checklist of no more than six steps.
Summarize the differences between the two proposed architectures discussed in this thread.
Write an executive summary of this annual report focusing on revenue and headcount.
Summarize the root cause and remediation of this postmortem in under 100 words.
Condense this API documentation page into a short cheat-sheet of the most-used endpoints.
Summarize the sentiment trend across these dated customer feedback entries.
Reduce this lecture transcript to a study outline with headings and sub-points.
Summarize the agreed next steps from this sprint retrospective.
Provide a short summary of this research paper's methodology and conclusion.
Summarize the competitive landscape described in this market analysis into a ranked list.
Extract the invoice number, total amount, and due date from this billing email.
Extract all email addresses mentioned in the following block of text.
Extract the action items and their assignees from these meeting notes.
Pull out every monetary figure and its currency from this financial statement.
Extract the start date, end date, and location from this event description.
Extract the list of dependencies and their pinned versions from this requirements file.
Identify and extract all phone numbers from this contact sheet, normalized to E.164.
Extract the key-value pairs from this semi-structured log line into JSON.
Pull the names and titles of every person quoted in this press release.
Extract the shipping address and tracking number from this order confirmation.
Extract every URL in this document and label each as internal or external.
Extract the SLA thresholds (uptime, response time) stated in this service agreement.
Identify and extract the error codes and their descriptions from this troubleshooting guide.
Extract the ingredient list and quantities from this recipe into a structured table.
Pull out the API rate limits described anywhere in this documentation.
Extract the company names and funding amounts from this list of funding announcements.
Extract the dates and the corresponding metric values from this performance log.
Identify and extract all the hashtags and @-mentions from these social posts.
Extract the section headings and page numbers to build a table of contents.
Extract the latitude/longitude coordinate pairs referenced in this field report.
Classify this customer message as one of: billing, technical, account, or feedback.
Label the sentiment of this product review as positive, neutral, or negative.
Classify this email as spam or not spam and give a one-line reason.
Categorize this support ticket by urgency: low, medium, high, or critical.
Determine whether this code change is a bugfix, feature, refactor, or docs change.
Classify the topic of this news headline into one of: politics, tech, sports, business.
Label this log line by severity: debug, info, warning, or error.
Classify this user query as informational, navigational, or transactional intent.
Decide whether this paragraph is written in a formal or informal register.
Classify this image caption as describing a person, place, object, or event.
Tag this issue with the most relevant component label from a given list.
Determine whether this transaction is likely fraudulent or legitimate, with a confidence note.
Classify the programming language of this code snippet.
Label this feedback as a feature request, bug report, or general praise.
Classify the difficulty of this interview question as easy, medium, or hard.
Decide whether this contract clause is favorable, neutral, or unfavorable to the buyer.
Classify this medical-leaflet sentence as a dosage, a side effect, or a warning.
Categorize this expense line into travel, meals, software, or other.
Label whether this PR description adequately explains the change: yes or no.
Classify the primary emotion expressed in this short message.
Three friends split a $73.50 bill so that one pays double the others; how much does each pay?
If all Bloops are Razzies and all Razzies are Lazzies, are all Bloops definitely Lazzies? Explain.
A train leaves at 2:15 PM going 60 mph; another leaves the same station at 3:00 PM going 80 mph on the same track. When does the second catch the first?
You have a 3-liter and a 5-liter jug; describe the steps to measure exactly 4 liters.
Given the sequence 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, what is the next term and why?
A store offers 20% off then an additional 10% off; is that the same as 30% off? Justify.
If it takes 5 machines 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how long for 100 machines to make 100 widgets?
Decide whether the argument 'It rained, so the ground is wet; the ground is wet, so it rained' is valid.
A farmer must cross a river with a fox, a chicken, and grain; outline a safe crossing plan.
Given a 12% annual interest rate compounded monthly, what is the effective annual rate?
Which is heavier: a kilogram of feathers or a kilogram of steel? Explain the trick.
If today is Wednesday, what day of the week will it be 100 days from now?
You flip a fair coin 3 times; what is the probability of getting at least two heads?
A rectangle's length is twice its width and its perimeter is 60; find its area.
Reason about whether a self-referential statement 'this sentence is false' can be true or false.
Given five people shaking hands once with each other, how many handshakes occur in total?
If a project is 60% done after 9 days at a constant rate, how many total days will it take?
Determine the missing number: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, __, and explain the rule.
A car's value drops 15% each year; after how many years is it worth less than half?
Explain step by step why the sum of the first n odd numbers equals n squared.
